body:
## Why this matters

#54 explicitly includes fleet-client contract work sourced from `projectbluefin/bluespeed#5`, `#16`, and `#32`, but no child issue currently owns that boundary. If it stays implicit, homelab node validation will blur into contributor-laptop management and later storage/service issues will start assuming clients are cluster nodes.

## Scope

Define the fleet-client follow-up under #54:

- make the client-vs-cluster split explicit for this repo: ghost and cluster homelab nodes versus contributor clients
- define what bootc-style client participation this repo should validate: image consumption, update/reporting/telemetry behavior, without enrolling laptops as k3s or KubeVirt nodes
- describe the minimum evidence model the lab should emit to distinguish cluster members from clients in later validation lanes
- split any dashboard, MDM, or broader fleet-product follow-up instead of hiding it inside the first substrate pass

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] The client-vs-cluster contract is explicit and aligned with the parent epic
- [ ] The issue states what bootc-style client behavior this repo should validate and what it should not
- [ ] Non-goals such as laptop kubelet enrollment or generic workstation-fleet management are stated directly
- [ ] The dependency between this contract and future storage/service lanes is linked explicitly

## Links

- Child of #54
- Can run in parallel with the provisioning follow-up after #56
- Prevents scope bleed into #52 and #51
- Source ideas: `projectbluefin/bluespeed#5`, `projectbluefin/bluespeed#16`, `projectbluefin/bluespeed#32`
title: feat: define fleet-client contract for bootc clients versus homelab nodes
